items.title: TDCJ official confirms in trial testimony that it could cost $1.5 billion to fully cool Texas prisons
items.desc: Current and former heads of Texas’ prison system defended asking for lesser amounts to air-condition facilities during a federal trial on extreme heat in TDCJ facilities.

items.title: Texas’ ban on smokeable hemp cannabis is in effect. Here’s what you need to know.
items.desc: New rules from state health officials ban smokeable intoxicating hemp products, including pre-rolled joints and hemp flower.

items.title: Texas will launch its own clinical trials into ibogaine psychedelic after failing to find a drug company to help
items.desc: The Legislature last year approved $50 million to research the psychedelic and find a company to develop it into a drug for FDA approval.

items.title: Texas judge rejects push to let churches make political endorsements
items.desc: A group of Texas churches and the IRS agreed to nix the Johnson Amendment, which prohibits nonprofits from endorsing candidates. A Trump-appointed judge said no.

items.title: Photos: The last day Texas stores can sell smokeable hemp cannabis
items.desc: New state rules that went into effect March 31 further restricts the amount of THC that can be found in hemp-derived products, effectively banning smokeables.

items.title: QatarEnergy, ExxonMobil Start Production at Texas LNG Project
items.desc: Golden Pass LNG expects to ship its first cargo in the second quarter.

items.title: EIA Launches Pilot Studies to Assess Data Center Energy Demand
items.desc: The U.S. Energy Information Administration announced that it is launching three voluntary pilot field studies to evaluate energy consumption in data centers.

items.desc: National Grid and Iberdrola awarded a GBP 3 billion ($3.96 billion) contract to Siemens Energy for Eastern Green Link 4 converter stations.
